text:
Cricketing legend turned commentator Mark Taylor has hit back after "naysayers" skewered his diving commentary at the 2024 Paris Olympics.

Aussie cricketing legend and former captain turned commentator Mark Taylor has lashed out at the “naysayers” lambasting his diving commentary at the Olympic Games.

Taylor, 59, along with Channel 9 copped backlash after he was appointed to commentate on the diving at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games.

Media veteran Tim Bailey, who has spent decades covering sport, weather and general news with Channel 10, lashed out at the move, going as far as to call for the executive producer in charge to be sacked.

“Mark Taylor calling the diving. A wonderful cricketer. But a little disrespectful to the athletes. Tubby probs knows nothing about this sport. He’s admitted as much. The EP who made this decision? Your last. #bellyflop,” he wrote on X, formerly Twitter.

The criticism was put to Taylor in an appearance on 2DAY FM’s Hughesy, Ed & Erin on Monday where he initially seemed unfazed by the backlash.

“Do you care in the slightest about that kind of stuff?” asked co-host Erin Molan.

Taylor replied: “No, not really."

“I don’t tend to follow the media all that closely, particularly social media, but I knew when I said I’d do this that there’d be people, the naysayers, who would say I shouldn’t be doing this.”

Mark Taylor looks like a fish out of water here#Diving#OlympicGames#ParisOlympics2024#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/yBYxfYAMis

Taylor continued to recall his daughter coming home from her teaching job where someone parroted Bailey's call to describe Taylor’s commentary as “disrespectful to the athletes,” incensing the former cricket star.

“I don’t accept that because I’ve met all the athletes and they were all delighted that I put my hand up to do it,” he said to the unanimously agreeing hosts.

“And Sam Fricker is the expert, I’m not a diver. I’m there to do my job which is to give you the scores which is a bit like cricket. It’s not disrespectful, that’s absolute rubbish.”

Bailey’s quip at Taylor’s diving commentary was not a standalone remark with outraged social media users skewering the appointment of the cricket specialist to the diving.

AAP sports reporter Oliver Caffrey likened the former cricketer’s commentary to a “12th man bit” while one X user accused Channel 9 of “taking the piss”.

Mark Taylor commentating on the diving sounds like a 12th Man bit

“'Sensational dive, that' - Probably… Former Australian cricket captain Mark Taylor commentating the diving. Channel 9 absolutely taking the piss with their Olympics team providing jobs for the boys and girls who have no business being there,” they wrote.

While the Olympics has been full of on-the-field competition, drama over the commentators off-the-field has proved to be just as lively.

EuroSport commentator Bob Ballard was sacked from his position earlier this week after making a sexist comment over the Australian women’s 4x100m freestyle relay team.

"Well, the women just finishing up. You know what women are like… hanging around, doing their make-up," he said as Emma McKeon, Shayna Jack, Mollie O'Calloghan and Meg Harris were making their way out of the Paris Aquatic Centre.

After being relieved of his commentary obligations by EuroSport, Ballard broke his silence to apologise for the remark but insisted he meant no offence to the female athletes.

“The comments I made during the Australian freestyle relay victory ceremony on Saturday have caused some offence,” he said via social media.

“It was never my intention to upset or belittle anyone and if I did, I apologies. I am a massive advocate of women's sport.

“I shall miss the Eurosport team dearly and wish them all the best for the rest of the Olympics.”
